note: 3 markets researched. TRADE candidate: KXPLATNERDROPOUT-26-JUL8 (NO lean, all 4 blind estimators + both round-2 reps below market). SKIP KXFEDDECISION-26JUL-H0: blind consensus 0.76 rested on a Jul-4 CME FedWatch reading (75.6% hold) that could not be refreshed to Jul-6; the live $2.7M-OI market at 0.84 is the fresher aggregate of the same fed-funds futures, so the arbiter invokes the half-anchor exception (verification cast doubt on the blind fact's currency), re-anchors to ~market (0.83), and kills the trade as spurious stale-data edge. SKIP KXBTCMAXMON-BTC-26JUL31-6500000: the digest mis-framed the settlement clause as a cumulative monthly trimmed-mean crossing $65k; the strike ladder ($65k->0.84, $75k->0.09) reveals it is a maximum/touch market on the BRTI index, so all four BTC blind estimates (0.30-0.58) are contaminated garbage-in. Corrected read (BTC ~+4-5% touch over 25 days) lands ~0.85, on top of market, no edge. Excluded per PROTOCOL iron-rule #4 (skip markets whose settlement was misread rather than force-estimate).

items.title: Will Graham Platner drop out of the 2026 Maine U.S. Senate election before Jul 8, 2026?
items.rationale: Core: all four blind estimators (Opus 0.20/0.30, Codex 0.40/0.24) and both round-2 reps (Opus 0.34, Codex 0.36, each capped by the half-anchor) landed below the 0.41 market, so consensus fair YES ~0.35 -> buy NO (fair 0.65 vs ask 0.60). Thesis: the market appears to compress 'a well-placed source says he plans to drop THIS WEEK' into 'drops by Jul 7'; Maine statute lets the party replace a withdrawing post-primary nominee only if withdrawal is by 5pm on Jul 13, so the rational exit-probability mass spreads across Jul 7-13, not Jul 7. Risk (bull case): postponing ALL events this week + a fresh Jul-6 Politico sexual-assault allegation can precede an imminent Tuesday announcement, and 'reflecting on the best path forward' can be pre-withdrawal language. Divergence: none material -- family split only 0.02; the disagreement is with the market on the date boundary, not between the models. Verification (Jul 6, multiple outlets: NBC/NPR/Axios/CNBC/The Hill/PBS): NO withdrawal announced as of Jul 6; he explicitly says he is still reflecting and calls the allegation false -- consistent with the blind facts, no new material info to re-estimate on.

items.title: Will the Federal Reserve maintain rates (hike 0bps) at the July 29, 2026 meeting?
items.rationale: Core: all four blind estimators anchored to CME FedWatch (~75.6% hold as of Jul 4; tail is a hike, not a cut) -> blind consensus 0.76. Risk: that anchor is a 2-day-old snapshot and could NOT be refreshed to a Jul-6 reading via search. Divergence: our 0.76 sits 8pts below the live 0.84 market. Arbiter judgement (KILL, not create): an 8-point standing basis between Kalshi and CME fed-funds futures on a $2.7M-OI binary is implausible; the market is far more likely the fresher FedWatch aggregate than free edge. Half-anchor exception invoked (blind fact's currency invalidated by verification) -> re-anchor to ~0.83, edge ~0.01, NO TRADE.
